Ophelia is there to greet you with a wide smile on her face. As she gets close enough, she suddenly hugs you tightly and after an awkwardly long moment, she lets go of you. “Sorry, I’m just so happy, Verusha means a lot to me,” after another moment she realizes what it sounds like. “N-not like that, we’re just friends.” You make small talk with her as you ride the elevator to the medical center. Entering Verusha’s room, you’re happy to see her sitting upright. Surprisingly, despite this being a medical facility. She has a lit cigarette between her teeth. “Verusha I thought you quit smoking,” Ophelia says, sounding disappointed. “I did, and I started again.” she snaps at the gryvain. She then looks at you and asks: “You here to complain about my smoking too?” “Nice to see you too,” you respond. Her ears fold back and she looks downwards. “I didn’t mean to be a dick,” she mumbles to the sheets Lightly grabbing her shoulder, you force her to look at you. “[pc.nmh |I know you don’t mean it, |Verusha if I took half of the shit you say seriously I wouldn’t be here, |It’s fine, I’m an ass too, I can take it, ]” you assure her. The corners of her mouth curls upwards slightly. “So, why are you here?” she asks. Ophelia speaks before you can. “We heard that you had recovered and wanted to make sure you’re alright.” She pauses for a moment before blurting out: “And we want to know what happened on the mission.” Verusha chuckles as she blows acrid smoke. “What, you can’t wait for the company to release the report?” “Come on, Verusha, you know the brass takes forever to get through those things, and even then they redact out all the best parts.” “Fine, just don’t go gossiping around. They look the other way, but we don’t want to push our luck.” She squashes the cigarette butt and readjusts her seating. “The Job we got pulled into was rescuing some kid. Parents with more money than sense. Our orders were to get him back. Intel suggested pirates or slavers, and it wasn’t that far off, but we underestimated the threat. Before we had a chance to properly react, they were boarding us. A couple of the bastards were shot down, but we were still ridiculously outnumbered.” “Didn’t take you for the surrendering type,” Ophelia teases. “Who said anything about surrendering? They weren’t just some two bit slavers. The boarders filled our ship with gas, knocked out most of the first response, and those who got their masks on were taken down with non-lethal weapons. They then flew our ships to their base. I woke up butt naked in a cell, but I wasn’t there for long. We were corralled into a large room with a screen. Some dzaan chick appeared on it, explaining why she had captured us.” “Bringing a large group of mercenaries into your base sounds like a recipe for disaster,” Ophelia laughs. “Well, she explained to us that she ran an illegal gladiatorial arena, so it must have seemed like the perfect idea.” Looking at Verusha, you realize that the topic is bothering her. Without being too confrontational, you ask her if she wants to keep talking about it. She thinks for a moment before sighing: “No, I have to face what I did, even if I was forced to do it.” “Verru, don’t push yourself,” the gryvain consoles her, gripping her gently by the shoulder. “We had to fight each other. Civilians, soldiers, pirates, everyone was treated the same.” Her voice falters as she speaks, but she pushes on. “If we wouldn’t, or didn’t perform well enough... like she put it: those who don’t pull in the ratings, get cancelled.” “You had no real choice. If you had refused, they would have just killed you and someone else would have taken your place,” you tell her reassuringly. “I know it’s just...” she trails off, not finding the words she wants to put forward. “So, how did you manage to get out of there?” Ophelia asks, wanting to move on from the topic. “The place was at a boiling point. People talking about rebellion. I guess we were just the spark that exploded the whole thing. As another show was about to start, we opened fire on the guards. If it was just us, we wouldn’t have made it out, but we were just a decoy. While we were dealing with the guards, the rest of the prisoners stormed the armory and the hangar. Even though we were supposed to fall back, I pushed in further, into the boss’ office —which is why I got injured so badly—.” “What happened to her? The Dzaan I mean,” Ophelia asks. “She’s alive, in Gastigoth.” You’re honestly surprised that she spared her, you didn’t take her for the merciful kind. Ophelia seems to think the same and actually voices the question of why she spared her. “I... I don’t know, Maybe I meant to use her as a hostage, or maybe I wasn’t thinking clearly, but I dragged her back to the ships. It turned out to be a good decision too, as they weren’t too keen on blowing up the person paying their bills. I don’t remember what happened after the take-off, since I passed out shortly after.” “Well, we’re glad you’re safe,” Ophelia says, patting the hyena mercenary on the shoulder. Before you can say anything, the doctor steps inside and ushers you out, telling you that you can come back later. Ophelia accompanies you down to the ship. original: Just like last time, you have no trouble getting aboard the merc ship. And once again Ophelia is waiting for you in the hangar. Walking behind her, you ask why she was waiting for you. “Don’t take it personally, it’s just that all civilians need to be escorted, so they don’t wander anywhere they shouldn’t be.” Fair enough. The elevator doors dings open as you arrive at the medical deck. The nurse at the desk greets you and tells you two that Verusha should be recovering from another surgery any time now, and you can wait in the room. It’s silent except for the steady beeping of the heartbeat monitor, and Verusha’s breathing. Sitting there, you doze off for a few minutes, but when Verusha wakes up with a groan you’re shaken from your dazed state. “Verusha, are you ok?” you ask, as you walk next to the bed. “Yeah, just a bit drowsy from the anesthetics. Could you get me some water?” she asks, rather politely, to your mild surprise. You step outside the room and get her a cup of water. As you return, she finishes reattaching her leg. “Thanks,” Verusha says, as you hand it to her. She empties it and looks at you. “Oh, and [pc.name], I’ve been meaning to talk to you.” Behind you, Ophelia makes a stealthy exit, giving the two of your privacy. “I’ve been thinking about this for a while, before the botched job. I-” before she can finish her sentence, she gasps for breath. Her heart starts beating faster and faster. Clearly something is wrong, so you rush out of the room and tell the first person you see that something’s wrong with Verusha. You, Ophelia, the doctor, and a couple of nurses rush back into Verusha’s room. Her heart is still beating very fast, her muscles are tensing, and her pupils are dilated. As the doctor moves closer to try to figure out what’s wrong, she suddenly springs to life, throwing the doctor across the room. Struggling against her, she recognizes you. |You try to pull her hands off of your neck, but she’s too strong. You’re starting to black out, but before you pass out, she loosens the grip on you. ] ] “Steele?” she asks, sounding confused. Suddenly Ophelia tackles her, and before she can beat her back, the doctor sticks a needle in her arm, putting her to sleep. “Wha- what the hell was that!?” Ophelia pants. “I have no idea,” the doctor responds. She grabs Verusha by the knees and Ophelia grabs her by the shoulders, and they lift her onto the bed. She takes a needle out of her pocket and draws some blood from her arm. “You should get out of here,” she says to you. “We’ll need to keep her sedated until we figure out why she went berserk.” “Come on, let’s go,” Ophelia says reassuringly as she leads you back to the hangar. “She’ll be fine, our medics have always pulled through in the end.” You can’t help but to worry about her. You’re sure she’ll be fine, but still.

original: Approaching the ship, you buzz on the intercom. After a while, a voice responds. “This is the captain of the Mercs-4-More transport ship echo. State your business.” “I’m [pc.name] Steele, here to visit Verusha,” you respond. “Please wait while I confirm with the brass.” After a few minutes, the radio buzzes with life again. “Alright, come aboard.” The door slides open and you step inside. “It’ll be a couple of hours until the guys get back with the supplies, so I’ll drop you off immediately,” the captain says over the intercom. You strap in as the engines come to life and the ship takes off. After a surprisingly short time the ship lands, and as you exit it you see that you’re now in a hangar with multiple ships bearing the same symbol. “Hey, Steele.” someone yells at you. Turning to look at the person calling your name, you see a dark-skinned gryvain. It takes you a good while to recognize her, but eventually you realize that she’s the mercenary you met on Tarkus when Verusha was taking you back to your ship. You wave back at her and step off the ship ramp. “Are you here to see Verusha?” she asks. “What else would I be here for?” you respond. “You never know. Come on, I’ll show you to the medical center,” she says with a little wave as she turns around. “Any improvements? Is she doing better?” you ask her as you exit the hangar and start walking down the corridor. “No, she’s still out cold, the docs are doing their best. They’ve pumped her full of anti-venoms and healing boosters. Now they’re waiting for her to recover enough to do more surgery.” she says in a flat tone of voice, but you detect a hint of worry in her voice. “So what exactly happened?” “Don’t know the details. I wasn’t in the team that was sent for the job,” she says as you two enter an elevator. “The reports are still classified and being reviewed.” The rest of the elevator ride is completely silent. You have to admit that you’re a bit worried. You know that Verusha is tough and has been through the wringer before, but this is the first time hearing that she’s out cold. Your thoughts are cut short when the elevator stops moving and the doors slide open. Stepping out of the elevator, you see that you’re in a lobby. “Hello, Ophelia,” the woman at the front desk greets you. “Hi, we’re here to see Verusha.” “She’s yet to recover consciousness, but if you’re sure, then go on ahead.” Ophelia leads you down one last corridor and to the room with Verusha. Your throat dries as you see Verusha laying there, unconscious, hooked up to machines and tubes. From the contour of the thin cloth covering her, you can see that her cybernetics have been removed. It really is a heart-wrenching sight. “She’ll be fine,” Ophelia assures you while placing a comforting hand on your shoulder. After a while, a doctor opens the door and informs you two that it’s time to leave as they need to perform more surgery on her. “Hey, Steele, don’t worry about her. It’ll take time, but she’ll be back up eventually,” Ophelia reassures you as the two of you make your way back to the hangar. “I’ll message you once her status improves,” she promises as you enter one of the transport ships.
